title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson claims Celtics' Derrick White is a top-five player in the NBA based on analytics.
Derrick White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, 5.7 assists, 1.2 steals, and 1.5 blocks per game this season.
White played a key role while Jayson Tatum was injured, contributing defensively and as secondary scorer for the Celtics.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 23 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists in a 109-98 win over the Cavaliers.
Jayson Tatum contributed 20 points in 27 minutes despite not being at full health, returning from an Achilles injury.
Derrick White praised for his influential play, highlighted by his high on/off rating and strong defensive contributions.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan advises against offering WR Alec Pierce a $30 million contract.
Patriots need a new top target for QB Drake Maye after releasing Stefon Diggs.
Alec Pierce excelled with the Colts, leading the NFL in yards per catch in 2024 and 2025.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown police officer Brittany Dougal died by suicide on March 4 after battling mental illness, the department announced.
Dougal, 29, graduated second in her class from the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy and joined the department in December.
A visitation and funeral for Dougal will be held in Danvers; donations are requested for North Kingstown Animal Shelter and Tunnel to Towers Foundation.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots considering under-the-radar free agents like Vikings WR Jalen Nailor and Browns LB Devin Bush for roster depth.
Jalen Nailor, 27, offers potential as a versatile receiver with 10 touchdowns over the last two seasons for the Vikings.
Browns linebacker Devin Bush had a resurgent 2025 with 125 tackles and could improve Patriots' depth at the position.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, minority owner of the Raiders, may have influenced the team's decision not to trade Maxx Crosby to the Patriots.
The Raiders traded Maxx Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th overall pick in the 2026 draft.
With the trade unavailable, the Patriots might explore free agency to strengthen their edge rusher position, targeting several potential candidates.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Alec Pierce, a Colts wide receiver, may earn at least $27 million per year if he hits free agency.
The Colts are negotiating to retain Pierce, with talks expected to go down to the wire before free agency.
Pierce led the NFL in yards per reception and had 1,003 yards and six touchdowns last season.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, 30, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Public Library.
Flynn faced dyslexia and ADHD, finding solace and support in libraries throughout her education and career.
Despite a rare vascular condition and four leg surgeries, Flynn is committed to completing the marathon challenge.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her patient Christina, who has advanced breast cancer.
Christina has completed 20 marathons and has qualified for the Boston Marathon 12 times despite her health challenges.
The Ellie Fund supports breast cancer patients by providing essential services to ease their treatment journey.

title: Here’s why a trade for Brian Thomas Jr. would be a home run move for the Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Brian Thomas Jr. could be a strategic trade option for the Patriots despite his down year in 2025.
Thomas offers potential value with 1,300 scrimmage yards in 2024 and a $4 million cost in 2026.
Trading for Thomas allows the Patriots financial flexibility to invest in other key positions.
